headline: Electrek earnings preview: 'Q1 2026 growth story is dead' — 50K inventory overhang, energy storage down 38% QoQ, sequential revenue decline expected; earnings call April 22 after close
Investors demand Optimus, FSD and robotaxi answers on tomorrow's Q1 2026 earnings call — AI5 timeline, Cybercab unit economics, and robotaxi miles-per-day growth are top 3 hotspot questions

Tesla Spring Update v2026.14+ pushes Robotaxi features to all owner vehicles — rear passenger display gets fully interactive navigation map while driving; capability previously reserved for Robotaxi fleet only

Musk: 'We expect fully autonomous vehicles in a quarter to half of the US' by end 2026 — robotaxis targeting 'dozens of major cities'; same promise made for 2025 and missed; aspirational language flagged by analysts

TSLA T-minus 24 hours to Q1 earnings: consensus $0.33 non-GAAP EPS / $21.4B revenue (Tesla official); 3 bull catalyst tests — Cybercab unit target, robotaxi revenue-per-mile, gross margin ex-credits >19%
14 Cybercab units WITHOUT steering wheels spotted at Giga Texas — production-spec autonomous Cybercabs rolling off line; original no-steering-wheel design confirmed; production ramp accelerating
~1 in 5 Cybertruck Q1 2026 registrations were internal SpaceX transfers — organic civilian retail demand materially weaker than headline numbers; bear case on Cybertruck profitability strengthened
Optimus robot live at Tesla Boston Boylston Street showroom for Marathon Monday — hundreds of thousands of spectators + international broadcast; zero ad spend PR at massive scale
Tesla launches unsupervised robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston — 573-vehicle Model Y fleet, no driver or monitor in front seats; first multi-city expansion beyond Austin confirmed live
Optimus Gen 3 hand and arm details revealed in new patents — new joint architecture and dexterity upgrades; production design locked per blueprint filings
